title: Day 3 · fs_safety boundary + ReadFileState hook
task: Use the read_file tool to read pyproject.toml and tell me the project name

card.label: Use read_file on pyproject.toml; tell me the project name

frames.caption: CLI parses the prompt and stuffs cwd into ToolContext. From today on, cwd isn't just a header line — it's the boundary circle for every file tool the model can call.

messages.content: Use read_file on pyproject.toml; tell me the project name

frames.caption: AnthropicProvider translates ToolRegistry.list() into input_schema and sends it to the Messages API. The model reads the tool list, decides to call read_file, and passes only a relative path.

messages.content: Use read_file on pyproject.toml; tell me the project name

frames.caption: Day 3's gate adds three checks over Day 1/2: resolve_in_cwd locks the relative path back into the cwd subtree (escape → ValueError); ensure_text_file uses a suffix allowlist or NUL-byte peek to reject binaries; ensure_within_size caps a single file at 256 KiB. Only after all three pass does the read happen.

messages.content: Use read_file on pyproject.toml; tell me the project name

frames.caption: Path.read_text reads pyproject.toml into memory, then truncate_output trims to 8000 chars. ReadFileState.record(path, content) stores (mtime_ns, char_count) — Day 4's read-before-edit will diff this record against the disk mtime to detect file drift between read and edit.

messages.content: Use read_file on pyproject.toml; tell me the project name

frames.caption: Day 3 also reshapes messages: append the assistant's tool_use message first, then bundle every tool_result from this round into one user message's content blocks — one batch per round, no longer one message per tool_use like Day 2.

messages.content: Use read_file on pyproject.toml; tell me the project name

card.label: final: The project name is agent-code.

frames.caption: AnthropicProvider calls the model a second time. The model reads the [project] block inside the tool_result, returns plain text with stop_reason=end_turn. The for step in range(max_steps) loop exits here.

messages.content: Use read_file on pyproject.toml; tell me the project name

card.label: stdout ← 'final: The project name is agent-code.'

frames.caption: CLI prints the three trace lines: tool_call, observation, final. The whole conversation and the ReadFileState are still in memory — Day 4's file_edit starts from this same ctx.read_state.

messages.content: Use read_file on pyproject.toml; tell me the project name
